_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of software related information technology (IT) security principles and methods (e.g., modularization, layering, abstraction, data hiding, simplicity/minimization).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.562), DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.544).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.4/10 (raw 0.343). Shared vocabulary: [data, knowledge, minimization, principles]. DCWF-only terms [abstraction, hiding, information, layering, methods, modularization] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, architecture, consent, default, design, limitation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.876 (P=0.873, R=0.878). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.5%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of software related information technology (IT) security principles an" and "Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including thei". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.588 > STS 0.343 (gap +0.244). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, knowledge, minimization, principles] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [data, knowledge, minimization, principles]. DCWF-only: [abstraction, hiding, information, layering, methods, modularization]. WIDAI-only: [application, architecture, consent, default, design, limitation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.5% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.343 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
STS 0.343 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.244 with low STS 0.343. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
